commit.message:
feat: show --help output in ati tool info for CLI tools (#71) (#72)

* feat: show --help output in ati tool info for CLI tools (#71)

CLI tools returned empty schema from `ati tool info` — agents had no
idea what arguments the tool accepts. Every other tool type (HTTP,
OpenAPI, MCP) returns a full schema.

Fix: wire the existing `capture_cli_help` function (which runs
`<command> --help` and captures stdout/stderr) into the `tool info`
display path. For CLI tools without an `input_schema`, `tool info`
now shows the captured --help output in both text and JSON modes.

Text mode:
  CLI Usage (from --help):
  Usage:  docker [OPTIONS] COMMAND
  ...

JSON mode:
  "help_text": "Usage:  docker [OPTIONS] COMMAND\n..."

Also improved the CLI tool display:
- Shows `Command: docker` instead of `Endpoint: GET` (meaningless for CLI)
- Shows `ati run docker -- <args>` usage hint instead of the HTTP-style flag list
- Removed stale `ati skill fetch read` reference from skills display

Tested against real docker, gh, gcloud, redis-cli on this machine.

Closes #71.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Greptile review — duplicate doc, stale fetch ref, JSON schema

Three P2s from Greptile on PR #72:

1. Duplicate doc comment on capture_cli_help — removed the old block
2. Stale `ati skill fetch read` hint in the proxy-mode tool info path
   (line 118) — removed to match the local-mode path
3. help_text always emitted in JSON for all tool types — now only
   included for CLI tools via conditional info["help_text"] insert

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

V4: eliminate slew-rate fallback branch entirely

The V3.1 branch had `prev_H = H` as escape hatch when profile_index
looked missing — that was the root cause of the B0<->H12 loop.
My previous V4 commit kept a residual fallback for INT32_MIN
(legacy sentinel), but that path is dead code: V3+ miners always
emit profile_index in block JSON, load_chain parses it, and
StoredBlock persists it.

Remove it: V4 strictly trusts stored_pi. If INT32_MIN ever reaches
the V4 branch (defensive), default to B0 (0) — conservative, not
disabling. The slew rate ±3 is now unconditional on every
transition at heights >= 4170.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sost-core
repository.description: ConvergenceX PoW engine for SOST protocol

commit.message:
HARD FORK V4 (block 4170): profile_index sentinel + Ahead Guard

Root cause of the B0<->H12 oscillation loop observed at blocks
4150-4166: BlockMeta::profile_index defaulted to 0, so V3.1 could
not distinguish a block legitimately mined at B0 from one whose
profile_index was never persisted. The V3.1 slew-rate branch
treated any stored 0 as missing and fell through to prev_H = H,
disabling the ±3 clamp — letting the next block jump straight to
H12.

Fix (activates at V4 fork height 4170, moved from 4200 to stop
the oscillation sooner):

- BlockMeta::profile_index default is now INT32_MIN. Legit B0 (0)
  is no longer confusable with "missing".
- StoredBlock persists profile_index per block; load_chain parses
  it from block JSON (presence-checked, not jint sentinel).
- All call sites that build std::vector<BlockMeta> from g_blocks
  now copy profile_index from StoredBlock.
- load_chain restores g_last_accepted_profile from the loaded tip.
- casert.cpp: new V4 branch uses INT32_MIN sentinel. Only genuinely
  missing values fall back; legit B0 enforces slew rate normally.
  V3.1 branch (4110-4169) preserved bit-for-bit for consensus.
- V4 Ahead Guard (bitsQ clamp at lag>=16) retained from previous
  V4 work — unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix launch lead: impulse-based speed estimate + TOF cap (v04.14)

Replace fudge-factor motor.maxSpd*0.6 with impulse/avgMass burnout
speed calculation, preventing absurd lead offsets for small motors
(e.g. H128W). Cap lead TOF at 20s to avoid nonsensical extrapolation.
Applied to both spawnInterceptor() and runHeadlessCombination().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable GSAP entrance animations on mobile — prevent invisible sections

gsap.from() with opacity:0 hides elements immediately, then relies on
ScrollTrigger to reveal them. On iOS Safari mobile, ScrollTrigger can
fail to fire, leaving entire sections blank. Fixed by gating animations
behind (min-width: 768px) so mobile cells/cards stay visible by default.

Affected: BentoGrid, WhyChooseUsSection

Fix admin inbox routing for collaboration requests

Store admin user_id in admin_config on panel visit.
Collaboration requests now look up admin_config first
before falling back to email search in users collection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full pipeline + v3 extraction: hippocampus paper scores 82 (top of 4 variants)

Ran the real 5-stage paper-builder skill (outline, plot, literature, writing,
refinement with table audit) on v3 inputs. Compared against prior variants:

Variant                   Inputs          Pipeline       Overall
-----------------------------------------------------------------
v2 single-shot (baseline) v2 qualitative  single-shot    77
v2 full pipeline          v2 qualitative  full 5-stage   74  (regressed)
v3 single-shot            v3 numeric      single-shot    80
v3 full pipeline (THIS)   v3 numeric      full 5-stage   82  (best)

Per-axis gain v3_real vs v2_paper baseline:
  Clarity:      83 -> 85  (+2)
  Rigor:        72 -> 84  (+12)  [weakest axis in n=100, now second-highest]
  Completeness: 81 -> 82  (+1)
  Writing:      84 -> 83  (-1)
  Presentation: 65 -> 80  (+15)  [weakest axis in n=100, now at ceiling]
  Citations:    79 -> 82  (+3)
  Overall:      77 -> 82  (+5)

Table audit: PASSED. No qualitative cell values in any results table.
All 4 figures included via \includegraphics. 35 verified references.
Statistical density dramatically improved: chi-squared with df, Welch's t
with fractional df, exact p-values (0.00022, 0.00031), exact proportions
(169/444, 1,581/4,928) preserved verbatim across 7 tables.

The two fixes (extraction + pipeline) are both needed and are additive:
- Pipeline alone (v2_real: 74) regressed because honest N/A cells score
  worse than qualitative words in LLM judging
- Extraction alone (v3_paper: 80) delivered the biggest gain
- Combined (v3_real: 82) is the best variant

This establishes a clean 4-cell factorial isolating extraction quality from
pipeline architecture. The n=100 benchmark was really measuring prompt
engineering against impoverished inputs; real quality gains come from
fixing the upstream data extraction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Frontend: safely stringify any object fields before rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: always set ephemeral:true on Strom flows; preserve ephemeral in template schema

Zod schemas for TemplateInput and TemplatePatch stripped the ephemeral field,
so any template created or updated via the API lost it — meaning activateStromFlow
never forwarded it to Strom.

Fix: add ephemeral to both Zod schemas so it round-trips correctly, and
unconditionally set ephemeral:true in flow-generator.ts (Open Live always
manages flow lifetime; the template's value is irrelevant).

chore(deploy): drop engines.node pin to support mixed-host deployment

Vercel requires Node >= 22 (18 is discontinued) while the ESS PWA build
pipeline consumes this code on Node 18. Removing the repo-level pin lets
each host set its own Node version externally (Vercel dashboard / ESS CI),
without forcing one to match the other.

Code runs unchanged on both Node 18 and 24.

commit.message:
Remove Tutorial 06 from hero meta, keep Group 11 only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite v2 extraction as two-phase v3 pipeline: lossless numeric preservation

Root cause of earlier benchmark limitation: the v2 extraction prompt literally
said "Do NOT copy sentences verbatim — paraphrase as research notes", which
caused the LLM to summarize quantitative results with "Higher"/"Lower"/"Enriched"
instead of preserving the actual numbers present in the source paper.

New two-phase design:
- Phase 1 (deterministic, regex + XML): extract all statistical sentences (F, t,
  chi-squared, p, r, %, n=, mean±SD, CI) + methods parameters (coordinates,
  volumes, titers, doses) + full tables (no cell truncation) verbatim. No LLM.
- Phase 2 (LLM, organization only): structure Phase 1 output into idea_summary
  and experimental_log. Rule changed from "paraphrase" to "preserve every
  numeric value verbatim — your job is to organize, not summarize."

Changes:
- Rewrite benchmark/scripts/llm_extract.py with Phase 1 regex extraction and
  Phase 2 prompt that includes extracted content block as ground truth
- Add benchmark/scripts/validate_extraction.py to measure numeric density
  and banned qualitative cells per paper
- Generate v3 extraction prompts for 5 test papers
- Run LLM Phase 2 to produce v3 markdown inputs

Results on 5 test papers (v2 vs v3 experimental_log):
  Numbers: 323 -> 965 (+198.8%)
  Statistical reports: 16 -> 102 (+537%)
  Banned qualitative cells: 42 -> 0 (eliminated)

Example (hippocampus-accumbens):
  v2: "Higher vs Lower" for place cell proportions
  v3: "169/444 neurons; 38%" vs "1,581/4,928 neurons; 32%; chi-squared p=0.012"

Also includes intermediate A/B test artifacts:
- skill_v2_improved papers (20 single-shot with stricter prompt — regressed -2.1)
- skill_v2_real paper (1 full pipeline run — regressed -3 due to N/A cells)
- batch_v2 prompts for the n=100 benchmark
- compare_improved.py A/B comparison script

Next step: regenerate a paper using v3 inputs to measure end-to-end impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `integrity-reactions` feature flag for MCPG reaction-based integrity promotion/demotion (#25948)

* Initial plan

* feat: add integrity-reactions feature flag for MCPG allow-only policy

Add new feature flag `integrity-reactions` that, when enabled, injects
`endorsement-reactions` and `disapproval-reactions` fields into the MCPG
allow-only integrity policy. Requires MCPG >= v0.2.18.

Changes:
- Add IntegrityReactionsFeatureFlag constant
- Add MCPGIntegrityReactionsMinVersion = "v0.2.18" constant
- Add new fields to GitHubToolConfig struct
- Parse new reaction fields in tools_parser.go
- Add mcpgSupportsIntegrityReactions() version gate helper
- Add injectIntegrityReactionFields() helper for both code paths
- Inject reactions into gateway allow-only policy (mcp_renderer_github.go)
- Inject reactions into DIFC proxy policy (compiler_difc_proxy.go)
- Add validateIntegrityReactions() validation
- Wire up validation in both compiler paths
- Update JSON schema with new fields
- Add comprehensive unit tests

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/sessions/24601d6f-99dd-4b19-ac56-7c90f187f8e9

Co-authored-by: lpcox <15877973+lpcox@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review comments - clean up incomplete comment and improve function doc

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/sessions/24601d6f-99dd-4b19-ac56-7c90f187f8e9

Co-authored-by: lpcox <15877973+lpcox@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(adr): add draft ADR-25948 for version-gated integrity reactions

Generated by Design Decision Gate workflow. Records the decision to
introduce the integrity-reactions feature flag with a semver version
gate (MCPG >= v0.2.18) and a shared injection helper across all
policy code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: make reaction lists optional with defaults, proxy-only enforcement

- endorsement-reactions defaults to [THUMBS_UP, HEART] when
  integrity-reactions feature flag is enabled
- disapproval-reactions defaults to [THUMBS_DOWN, CONFUSED]
- Reaction-based integrity is only enforced in proxy mode (DIFC/CLI
  proxy), not MCP gateway mode, because the GitHub MCP server protocol
  does not expose reaction author information
- Compiler emits a warning when reactions are configured with the
  gateway path
- Validation now requires min-integrity when feature flag is enabled
  (even without explicit reaction lists, since defaults will be injected)
- Schema updated: removed minItems constraint, added default values
- Updated all type docs and schema descriptions to clarify proxy-only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add password protection and block search engine indexing

Fix analysis feature: read transcript_id from form data instead of session

The /analyze route was attempting to retrieve transcript_id from session.get('current_transcript_id'), but this session key is never populated. Fixed by reading transcript_id from the POST form data (passed as hidden input field from result.html). This completes the analysis workflow so users can submit analysis prompts after transcription.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

audit: deep QC — go-flat not overfitted, cost is the real risk

QC-1 Go-flat overfitting:
  2015 crash: go-flat DID NOT trigger (classified as 下跌, not 震荡)
  Protection came from drawdown brake only (-17.3%→-11.3%)
  Overall: 48.5% of losing periods caught, 0% of winning periods killed
  Conclusion: NOT overfitted, but weak in fast crashes

QC-2 Liquidity:
  Hold-panel amount merge failed — needs fix for proper analysis

QC-3 Transaction costs (CRITICAL):
  Average turnover: 83% per rebalance
  At realistic 40bp one-way cost: ann_ret 31%→26.8%, calmar 1.35→1.13
  At 80bp (high cost): ann_ret→21.3%, calmar→0.86
  Annual cost burden: ~7% of AUM at current turnover

Módulo Mantenimiento: KPIs de tickets, cola con filtros por estado, asignación de proveedor, resolución con costo automático a gastos.

Módulo Reservas: badge de solicitudes pendientes, aprobar/rechazar con motivo, calendario mensual con colores por área.

Módulo Comunicaciones: plantillas rápidas (corte de agua, asamblea, etc.), selector de destinatarios, preview de email, historial con reenviar.

Módulo Guardias: vista semanal de turnos, asignación rápida, incidentes recientes, reporte de horas con tarifa editable y exportar Excel.

commit.message:
feat: step 8 — 反馈学习（feedback-worker + 偏好规则 + settings 页）

- 新增 feedback-worker 服务（端口 8002）：difflib diff + Claude Haiku
  提炼写作偏好规则，逐条写入 writing_memory
- POST /api/drafts/:id/feedback：保存定稿，同步调用 feedback-worker，
  返回 rules_extracted 数量
- 移除 POST /api/kb/memory/feedback 的 require_auth（内部 worker 调用）
- settings.py 新增模板 CRUD（GET/PUT/DELETE /api/settings/templates/:name）
- /settings 页完整实现：流程节奏 / 选题方向 / 模板管理 / 偏好规则列表
- /drafts 页新增"提交定稿"折叠区，提交后显示已学习规则数
- CLAUDE.md 记录第八步完成状态及 diff 学习的已知疑点

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

not just at the end, so no progress is ever lost.

commit.message:
feat: add multi-provider web search module (Serper → Brave → DDG) (#24)

Python port of daatan's webSearch.ts. New module web_search.py provides
search_articles(query, limit, date_from, date_to) → List[SearchResult]
with a Serper.dev → Brave → DuckDuckGo fallback chain.

Wired into gnews_ingest.py as a fallback step between GNews RSS and GDELT:
when GNews returns 0 articles for a cell, web search queries the source
domain directly with the event's ASCII keywords + date window.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add sitemap-seeded harvester from Hive Index (Phase 3B.2)

New harvester reads local hiveindex-sitemap.xml (4,351 community
slugs) without making any requests to thehiveindex.com. Two-pass
strategy: Pass 1 constructs Reddit URLs directly from r-* slugs
(free). Pass 2 uses Serper queries for remaining names. Registered
as one-time job (not on cron) — trigger via add_job. Sitemap file
copied into repo and included in Docker image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: actualizar dominio de Railway a aprende-excel.com en emails de stripe_webhook

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(entry): update AGENTS.md and README.md for kernel rewrite

Refresh the two top-level entry files to reflect the kernel
rewrite layout:

- AGENTS.md monorepo tree now leads with `src/kernel/` and its
  subsystem breakdown, demotes the old daemon to "deprecated,
  kept for reference", and adds `src/tui/` alongside `src/cli/`.
  Hard rules rewritten in frontend/kernel terms, and a pointer
  added to `src/kernel/docs/design.md`.
- README.md quickstart swaps `src/run-cli.sh` for `src/run-tui.sh`
  to match the current preferred frontend entry point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(typography): apply font-heading to all Title elements across sections

Ensures every section/card/item title renders in the secondary font
(Lustria for portfolio-law) instead of the body sans font. Also
defaults CardTitle atom to font-heading so all Card-based sections
inherit the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
